items.title: Strong lead for Jo Palmer, close race in Huon in Upper House elections

children.content: Voters in Rosevears have given Education Minister Jo Palmer a strong lead, while the race in the southern electorate of Huon is much closer with independent Dean Harriss fighting to retain his seat.

gallery.altText: woman in pink enters piece of paper into a white ballot box
a brunette woman surrounded by her family and pet dog speaks to cameras
A man wearing a green shirt and jeans smiles, placing paper into a ballot box.

items.title: Spike in number of Tasmanian police on leave due to mental health issues

children.content: More than 100 Tasmanian police officers are currently off duty due to mental health compensation claims, a number that has doubled in five years.

image.altText: Taser in holster of police officer

items.title: Smelter jobs at stake as government assistance runs dry

children.content: South Australia's premier says there is a "national imperative" to keep smelters running, as a $135 million assistance package for Nyrstar operations at Port Pirie and Hobart expires.

image.altText: An aerial view of the Nyrstar smelter in Port Pirie

items.title: Second of two new Spirit of Tasmania ferries arrives in Geelong

children.content: Following a seven-week journey from Scotland, the Spirit of Tasmania V has arrived in Geelong. It will undergo gantry testing tomorrow before being temporarily relocated to Station Pier in Melbourne.

gallery.altText: A large red and white ferry with SPIRIT OF TASMANIA on it sailing through a bay.

thumbnail.altText: A large red and white ferry with SPIRIT OF TASMANIA on it sailing through a bay.

items.title: CCTV offline during knife incident at youth detention centre

children.content: Security cameras at Ashley Youth Detention Centre were offline when a detainee threatened a staff member with a knife during a cooking workshop, documents reveal.

image.altText: Exterior photos of a youth detention centre.

items.title: A guide to the six books vying for the $60,000 Stella Prize

children.content: A memoir by Geraldine Brooks and a historical novel by actor-turned-author Tasma Walton are among the eclectic works up for one of Australia's most prestigious literary awards.

image.altText: Three female authors shown in front of the covers of their books.

items.title: What this truck full of fibreglass sheets tells us about building price pressures

children.content: On Australia's busiest freight route, it takes four shots of coffee, $2,553 of fuel and a little hair metal to keep the wheels of the economy turning.

gallery.altText: Sean Mulvaney wears gloves as he holds a petrol pump hose and fills up a blue and red truck that towers beyond the frame.

items.title: Jamarra sparks Suns after half-time as tackle leaves Giant concussed

children.content: Jamarra Ugle-Hagan makes his mark in the third quarter as the Gold Coast takes control against GWS. Follow live.

image.altText: Jamarra Ugle-Hagan roars in celebration after a goal

items.title: Marshall slams 'unacceptable' Tigers after horror second stanza

children.content: Benji Marshall said he could not recognise his Wests Tigers side after a humiliating second half saw Cronulla dominate at Shark Park, while there were also wins for Newcastle and Penrith on a bumper Sunday of NRL football.

image.altText: Wests Tigers look sad

items.title: 'I made the choice': Why James Valentine resolved to die on his own terms

children.content: Broadcaster James Valentine was offered two different treatments for his cancer, so he chose the one that let him keep doing what he loved. Australian Story followed Valentine over his final 12 months as he faced the end with courage, purpose and humour.

items.title: Liberals focus on 'lessons' for Victorian election after decisive Nepean win

children.content: It took less than three hours from the start of vote counting for election analysts to call the result in the Liberals' favour, with a win for former local mayor Anthony Marsh.

image.altText: A man with light red hair and glasses in a suit stands on a stage with his hands clasped while a blonde woman speaks into a mic.

items.title: A year after the landslide, Albanese faces a harsher reality

children.content: The prime minister's second term is now defined less by what he wants to do than by what unforeseen events will allow.

image.altText: anthony albanese holds up medicare card

items.title: 'Disgusted': Survivors speak out against video games depicting scam centres

children.content: Scam centre survivors say they are hurt by games that serialise scam compound experiences online, but one creator says he hopes people find the game "fun".

items.title: The cafe meeting, the $2m bounty and the botched hit that shocked Sydney

children.content: As the house of Hamzy began to fall one of its senior members hatched a million-dollar plan to take out rivals in revenge. What followed has been a secret for five years.

image.altText: Ibrahem Hamze 2

items.title: 'If the cops came, you'd slip out the back': The shifting role of Aussie pubs

children.content: From a tiny Riverina pub to a polished inner-city institution, Australia's local pubs are changing hands at record pace. As big money pours in, what happens to the communities that built them?

image.altText: Graphic illustration of arms and hands holding schooners of beer 'cheersing' under old wrought iron balcony decoration

items.title: Love story: An accident changed their lives but strengthened their bond

children.content: The goalposts moved dramatically on Beau and Lucy Vernon's life plans, but it brought them closer together.

gallery.altText: A woman in a white wedding dress standing next to a man using a mobility aid

thumbnail.altText: A woman in a white wedding dress standing next to a man using a mobility aid

items.title: A 17-year-old Go-Betweens song inspired Robert Forster's debut novel

children.content: The Go-Betweens frontman spent years developing the story, which follows a pair of musicians on the run from the law as they try to clear their names.

image.altText: A man in sunglasses and a t-shirt that says I don't do drugs. I do time is lit by a pink filter

items.title: 'Soundtrack to Australia': ARIA reveals six 2026 Hall of Fame inductees

children.content: For the first time in more than a decade, multiple artists are being inducted into the ARIA Hall of Fame.

image.altText: Two Tongan-Australian women pose in floral power suits, daisy yellow and powder blue respectively, on a marbled balcony

items.title: Toure scores again for Norwich in red hot preparation for World Cup

children.content: Mohamed Toure scores his 10th goal in just 12 matches for Norwich to keep him in red hot form ahead of the World Cup.

image.altText: A footballer swings his leg at the ball.

items.title: Aussies put Olympic heartbreak to bed with historic world title

children.content: Australia, despite not having a dedicated curling facility, has claimed a first ever world championship title, with Tahli Gill and Dean Hewitt victorious in Switzerland overnight.

image.altText: Tahli Gill and Dean Hewitt hug

items.title: Miami GP to start three hours early as storm fears grow

children.content: F1's governing body announces the Miami Grand Prix will start three hours early, as fears grow about torrential rain.

image.altText: A man walks in the pits holding an umbrella.

items.title: 'Inhumane': Mick's disgust at cops' justification over the tasering of his 95yo mum

children.content: In an in-depth interview ahead of the inquest into his mother Clare Nowland's nursing home death, Mick says the family wants changes that will prevent another tragedy.

image.altText: An older man wearing a short-sleeve black button up shirt stands in his backyard in front of a green fence and hedges.

items.title: Nomadic family says 'dream is more powerful' than surging fuel prices

children.content: While many Australians are thinking twice about big road trips due to fuel costs, the Sharavnyam family from Mongolia is determined to fulfil a dream to travel the world.

gallery.altText: A man and woman sit in the front seats of a camper van, while three kids lie on a bed built into the van.

thumbnail.altText: A man and woman sit in the front seats of a camper van, while three kids lie on a bed built into the van.

items.title: Australian says Gaza aid boat activists were beaten by Israeli navy

children.content: Zack Schofield was one of three Australians taken to hospital after being released by Israeli authorities, and says he saw at least 10 of his crew being shot by rubber bullets.

image.altText: A young man in a light brown jumper speaks to a camera he's holding up in a kitchenette.

items.title: Government announces program to unlock land for oil infrastructure

children.content: The state government has announced the Accelerating Fuel Infrastructure Program, which it says is intended to ease fuel costs and protect against future price shocks.

image.altText: A large ship pictured in Port of Brisbane.

items.title: King Charles won over Trump with his visit but not Zohran Mamdani

children.content: King Charles's US state visit hit a bump in New York when Mayor Zohran Mamdani said if the pair were to speak, he would "probably encourage him to return the Koh-i-Noor diamond".

image.altText: King Charles standing next to Queen Camilla leans in to listen to Zohran Mamdani.

items.title: Australian taxpayers take on risk for major oil and gas projects

children.content: Australian governments take on risk to get major resource projects up and running, so why don't they take an equity stake?

image.altText: Close up of a collection of jagged red rocks. In the background, two towers spitting flames.

items.title: Dancing queens return to Trundle ABBA Festival to celebrate pop icons

children.content: For one day each year, the town of 335 people swaps tractors for tribute acts, grain trucks for glitter and everyday life for an all-out ABBA extravaganza.

image.altText: A group of ladies dressed in pink costumes stand in front of an ABBA sign in the crowd

items.title: What a vote of confidence in NZ's prime minister could mean for the country's election

children.content: New Zealand Prime Minister Christopher Luxon has survived a vote of confidence in his leadership amid a drop in approval ratings. Here's what this could mean for NZ's upcoming election.

image.altText: Two men shake hands while the other man looks happy but nervous.

items.title: Calls for change after soccer club told it needs men's team to compete

children.content: Millicent United's women's side is flying this season, but without a men's team the club has been told they are unable to compete.

image.altText: Two women standing on a soccer pitch, holding a soccer ball.

items.title: Ukrainian vet saved humans and animals before finding refuge in Australia

children.content: Wildlife veterinarian Maria Mykytiuk led an idyllic life in Bucha, Ukraine, before Russian forces invaded the city in 2022.

image.altText: Ukrainian woman sits on ground surrounded by her young son and daughter. They are wrapped in a blanket covered in sunflowers.

items.title: Republicans air concerns after US announces 5,000 troops to leave Germany

children.content: Two Republicans, who hold positions on armed services committees, say they have concerns for NATO alliances and security after the Pentagon confirms the withdrawal of 5,000 US troops from Germany.

image.altText: Donald Trump speaking, his hand in the foreground.

items.title: Murray-Darling Basin's apex predator back from the brink

children.content: Murray cod numbers have bounced back across much of Victoria and NSW, and that's good news for the whole Murray-Darling Basin ecosystem.

gallery.altText: Close up of a Murray cod.

thumbnail.altText: Close up of a Murray cod.

items.title: Untold stories of women in whaling inspire art that delves into the past

children.content: The overlooked contributions of women in the whaling industry are the subject of an exhibition in the Great Southern region of Western Australia.

image.altText: eyes in a painting

items.title: North Queensland Games spark regional sport revival

children.content: The largest multi-sport event in regional Australia begins in Mackay, with 2,000 competitors flocking to the city from across Queensland.

image.altText: A girl and her mum stand shoulder to shoulder in front of a running track.
